hero.label: Made for Hadrius
hero.headline: I want to help Hadrius modernize compliance outreach at scale.
hero.subheadline: Hadrius stands out because it is solving a painful, high stakes problem for investment firms with an AI native compliance platform. I am excited by the chance to join as a BDR and help bring a product trusted by hundreds of financial institutions to even more firms managing serious regulatory complexity.

whyCompany.title: Compliance is painful, expensive, and perfect for automation
whyCompany.body: Hadrius is tackling securities compliance in a market where trust, speed, and audit readiness matter every day. Serving 400 plus institutions and supporting over $4T in AUM shows the problem is real, urgent, and valuable enough to win budget.

whyCompany.title: Founding BDR at a breakout stage
whyCompany.body: Hadrius is still early, YC backed, and already has meaningful traction with a growing team in New York. That makes this role exciting because outbound can directly shape pipeline, messaging, and how the company expands into more investment firms.

whyCompany.title: I have already built pipeline through outbound
whyCompany.body: At Copygen.ai, I ran cold outreach, managed HubSpot, built LinkedIn sequences, and helped acquire 15 to 20 paying clients. At NextAfter, I sharpened my digital marketing skills and learned how better messaging and targeting improve conversion, which is highly relevant for a BDR role at Hadrius.

quote: I want this role because Hadrius is selling something that matters deeply to its customers, not just something nice to have. I love outbound when the problem is urgent, the value is clear, and every conversation can open a real door.
